response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with ~15 years of experience, but the fit for this specific role is weak. His C++ exposure is minimal and dated (a 7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project), far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. He has no SQL Server or Oracle experience listed (MySQL/MongoDB only), and no fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets domain background — RIDE Capital was a German retail-investment startup, not an investment-banking environment. His recent work is TypeScript/Node.js product engineering, which does not map to the core requirements.
strengths: Long, consistent engineering career with strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing)
Experience with low-latency, high-throughput systems (DLang microservices handling millions of requests/sec)
Solid CI/CD, scripting, and DevOps tooling background, which matches the desirable skills list
concerns: C++ experience is brief (~7 months professionally, ~10 years ago) versus the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows
No fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets / investment banking domain experience
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MySQL and MongoDB

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain
Strong general engineering and performance-systems background
